title: Multiple-choice cloze
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.text: In recent years, researchers have become increasingly interested in how digital environments influence human attention. While early studies suggested that constant notifications merely distracted users, more recent findings (0) that the issue is far more complex.||One key factor is the way in which digital platforms are deliberately designed to (1) users’ natural cognitive tendencies. For instance, many apps rely on variable reward schedules, which are known to (2) habitual behaviour. This mechanism, originally observed in behavioural psychology, has now been (3) into the architecture of everyday technology.||However, some experts argue that blaming technology alone is an oversimplification. They point out that individuals must also learn to (4) responsibility for their own digital habits. Developing strategies to manage attention can help users (5) the negative effects of overstimulation.||Interestingly, certain studies indicate that short periods of distraction may even (6) creativity, provided they are followed by focused work. This suggests that the goal should not be to eliminate distractions entirely, but rather to (7) them in a way that supports productivity. Ultimately, understanding how attention operates in the digital age may allow society to (8) more balanced and intentional use of technology.

example.explanation: “Imply” collocates with abstract conclusions drawn from research. The others are too explicit or used for formal announcements, not research findings.

questions.explanation: “Cater to” means to accommodate or exploit tendencies. The others do not collocate with ‘cognitive tendencies’.
“Trigger habitual behaviour” is a common collocation. The others do not fit semantically with behaviour formation.
“Embedded into the architecture” is the correct fixed collocation. The others do not fit the context of system design.
The fixed phrase is “take responsibility for.” The other verbs do not form idiomatic expressions with ‘responsibility’ here.
“Lessen the effects” is the standard collocation. The others are not used with ‘effects’ in this sense.
“Foster creativity” is a common collocation. The others imply limiting or redirecting, which contradicts the meaning.
“Regulate distractions” fits the idea of managing them. The other verbs do not collocate with ‘distractions’ in this context.
“Cultivate use” meaning to develop or encourage a balanced approach. The others do not fit semantically with ‘use of technology’.
